# Name: # Dual-OTA layout sized to fill 4 MB flash exactly — 4 MB is the smallest part across
# the supported targets (esp32 / esp32s3 / esp32c3 / esp32c6)
# Each OTA slot is 0x1f0000 (~2.03 MB); the app ranges ~1.5 MB (esp32) to ~1.83 MB

Type: the largest — c6 pulls in extra radio stack)

# Name: # keeps ~190 KB headroom. A larger flash (e.g. 8 MB S3) just leaves the top unused
# ONE table serves every board. esp_https_ota writes the INACTIVE slot
# never touch nvs (0x9000) → WiFi / VIN / key survive upgrades. otadata tracks the active
# slot; a blank otadata (after a full erase) boots ota_0. ota_0 stays at 0x20000 so a
# device already on the old 8 MB table keeps OTA-updating without a reflash (the INSTALLED
# table governs OTA writes; new full-flashes get this 4 MB table).
